However, the present observation is informative in the sense that the public signal’s precision can be varied through σε2superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜀2\sigma_{\varepsilon}^{2} which is independent of the other parameters. where αz→∞→subscript𝛼𝑧\alpha_{z}\rightarrow\infty, there exist multiple equilibria in strategies.

  3. 3.

    Multiple equilibria are ensured in the limit where αx→∞→subscript𝛼𝑥\alpha_{x}\rightarrow\infty.

  4. 4.

    Finally, in the special case where σμ2=0subscriptsuperscript𝜎2𝜇0\sigma^{2}_{\mu}=0 and αp=0subscript𝛼𝑝0\alpha_{p}=0, condition (38) collapses into the uniqueness condition 2​π≤αxαz2𝜋subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑧\sqrt{2\pi}\leq\frac{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}{\alpha_{z}}, of Angeletos and Werning, (2006) pp. 1733-1734, which is nested in the present framework.

Appendix D Alternative Derivation of (7)

This appendix contains a derivation of (7) which “explicitly” accounts for the influence which the prior’s distribution has on the critical mass condition. Recalling the PIC, we have:

P​(θ≤θ∗|x∗,μ)=c⇔Φ​(α​(θ∗−αxα​x∗−αpα​μ))=c;α=αx+αp.formulae-sequence𝑃𝜃conditionalsuperscript𝜃superscript𝑥𝜇𝑐⇔formulae-sequenceΦ𝛼superscript𝜃subscript𝛼𝑥𝛼superscript𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝𝛼𝜇𝑐𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝\displaystyle P(\theta\leq\theta^{*}|x^{*},\mu)=c\quad\Leftrightarrow\quad\Phi\Big{(}\sqrt{\alpha}(\theta^{*}-\frac{\alpha_{x}}{\alpha}x^{*}-\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha}\mu)\Big{)}=c;\quad\alpha=\alpha_{x}+\alpha_{p}. (40)

Regarding the CMC, we now account explicitly for the prior’s distribution and write:

A​(x∗​(μ),θ∗)=P​(x≤x∗|θ∗)=θ∗⇔∫−∞∞Φ​(αx​(x∗​(μ)−θ∗))​ϕ​(μ)​𝑑μ=θ∗,formulae-sequence𝐴superscript𝑥𝜇superscript𝜃𝑃𝑥conditionalsuperscript𝑥superscript𝜃superscript𝜃⇔superscriptsubscriptΦsubscript𝛼𝑥superscript𝑥𝜇superscript𝜃italic-ϕ𝜇differential-d𝜇superscript𝜃\displaystyle A(x^{*}(\mu),\theta^{*})=P(x\leq x^{*}|\theta^{*})=\theta^{*}\quad\Leftrightarrow\quad\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\Phi\Big{(}\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}\Big{(}x^{*}(\mu)-\theta^{*}\Big{)}\Big{)}\phi(\mu)d\mu=\theta^{*}, (41)

where Φ​()Φ\Phi() represents the cumulative of the standard normal distribution and ϕ​(μ)italic-ϕ𝜇\phi(\mu) is the normal density of the prior. To show that the pairs x∗​(μ),θ∗superscript𝑥𝜇superscript𝜃x^{*}(\mu),\theta^{*}, which solve (40) and (41) are unique if (αxαp)2+σμ2≥12​πsuperscriptsubscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝2subscriptsuperscript𝜎2𝜇12𝜋\sqrt{(\frac{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}{\alpha_{p}})^{2}+\sigma^{2}_{\mu}}\geq\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}, we solve (40) for the threshold level x∗=−Φ−1​(c)​ααx+θ∗​ααx−αpαx​μsuperscript𝑥superscriptΦ1𝑐𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥superscript𝜃𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥𝜇x^{*}=-\Phi^{-1}(c)\frac{\sqrt{\alpha}}{\alpha_{x}}+\theta^{*}\frac{\alpha}{\alpha_{x}}-\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}\mu, and substitute x∗superscript𝑥x^{*} into (41) to obtain a one-dimensional equation in θ∗superscript𝜃\theta^{*} alone:

∫−∞∞Φ​(αx​(−Φ−1​(c)​ααx+θ∗​ααx−αpαx​μ−θ∗))​ϕ​(μ)​𝑑μ=θ∗.superscriptsubscriptΦsubscript𝛼𝑥superscriptΦ1𝑐𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥superscript𝜃𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥𝜇superscript𝜃italic-ϕ𝜇differential-d𝜇superscript𝜃\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\Phi\Big{(}\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}\Big{(}-\Phi^{-1}(c)\frac{\sqrt{\alpha}}{\alpha_{x}}+\theta^{*}\frac{\alpha}{\alpha_{x}}-\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}\mu-\theta^{*}\Big{)}\Big{)}\phi(\mu)d\mu=\theta^{*}.

The sufficient condition for uniqueness of the threshold θ∗superscript𝜃\theta^{*} is therefore:

αpαx​12​π​∫−∞∞e−(αx​(−Φ−1​(c)​ααx+θ∗​αpαx−αpαx​μ))2​12​ϕ​(μ)​𝑑μ≦1.subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥12𝜋superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑒superscriptsubscript𝛼𝑥superscriptΦ1𝑐𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥superscript𝜃subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥𝜇212italic-ϕ𝜇differential-d𝜇1\displaystyle\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{-\Big{(}\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}\Big{(}-\Phi^{-1}(c)\frac{\sqrt{\alpha}}{\alpha_{x}}+\theta^{*}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}-\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}\mu\Big{)}\Big{)}^{2}\frac{1}{2}}\phi(\mu)d\mu\leqq 1. (42)

To obtain the final condition, we recall that μ∼𝒩​(E​[μ],σE​[μ])similar-to𝜇𝒩𝐸delimited-[]𝜇subscript𝜎𝐸delimited-[]𝜇\mu\sim\mathcal{N}(E[\mu],\sigma_{E[\mu]}), and use the moment generating function for the non-central χ2superscript𝜒2\chi^{2} distribution in Paragraph 1) to rewrite (42), as:

αpαx​12​π​11+(σμ​αpαx)2​e−12​(αx​(−Φ−1​(c)​ααx+θ∗​αpαx−αpαx​E​[μ]))2​11+(σμ​αpαx)2≤1.subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥12𝜋11superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜇subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥2superscript𝑒12superscriptsubscript𝛼𝑥superscriptΦ1𝑐𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥superscript𝜃subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥𝐸delimited-[]𝜇211superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜇subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥21\displaystyle\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+(\sigma_{\mu}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}})^{2}}}e^{-\frac{1}{2}\Big{(}\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}\Big{(}-\Phi^{-1}(c)\frac{\sqrt{\alpha}}{\alpha_{x}}+\theta^{*}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}-\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}E[\mu]\Big{)}\Big{)}^{2}\frac{1}{1+(\sigma_{\mu}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}})^{2}}}\leq 1. (43)

Taking logarithms yields the uniqueness condition (αxαp)2+σμ2≥12​πsuperscriptsubscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝2subscriptsuperscript𝜎2𝜇12𝜋\sqrt{(\frac{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}{\alpha_{p}})^{2}+\sigma^{2}_{\mu}}\geq\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}.

1) Moment Generating Function

Regarding (42), we recall our assumption that μ∼𝒩​(E​[μ],σE​[μ])similar-to𝜇𝒩𝐸delimited-[]𝜇subscript𝜎𝐸delimited-[]𝜇\mu\sim\mathcal{N}(E[\mu],\sigma_{E[\mu]}). We can therefore define y=−Φ−1​(c)​ααx+θ∗​αpαx−αpαx​μ𝑦superscriptΦ1𝑐𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥superscript𝜃subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥𝜇y=-\Phi^{-1}(c)\frac{\sqrt{\alpha}}{\alpha_{x}}+\theta^{*}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}-\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}\mu, where y∼𝒩​(−Φ−1​(c)​ααx+θ∗​αpαx−αpαx​E​[μ],σμ​αpαx)similar-to𝑦𝒩superscriptΦ1𝑐𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥superscript𝜃subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥𝐸delimited-[]𝜇subscript𝜎𝜇subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥y\sim\mathcal{N}(-\Phi^{-1}(c)\frac{\sqrt{\alpha}}{\alpha_{x}}+\theta^{*}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}-\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}E[\mu],\sigma_{\mu}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}). If a variable y𝑦y is normally distributed with mean E​[y]𝐸delimited-[]𝑦E[y] and variance σy2subscriptsuperscript𝜎2𝑦\sigma^{2}_{y}, then z2=y2σy2superscript𝑧2superscript𝑦2subscriptsuperscript𝜎2𝑦z^{2}=\frac{y^{2}}{\sigma^{2}_{y}} is non-centrally χ2superscript𝜒2\chi^{2} distributed. We can therefore use the moment generating function for the non-central χ2superscript𝜒2\chi^{2} distribution (Rao, (1965), p. 181):212121𝔼​et​z2=12​π​∫−∞∞et​z2​e−(z−E​[z])22​𝑑z=12​π​∫−∞∞e−(1−2​t)​z2−2​E​[z]​z+E​[z]22​𝑑z=12​π​∫−∞∞e−(1−2​t​z−E​[z])2−E​[z]21−2​t+E​[z]22​𝑑z=11−2​t​2​π​∫−∞∞e−E​[z]2​t1−2​t​e−(1−2​t​z−E​[z])22​d​(1−2​t​z)=11−2​t​e−E​[z]2​t1−2​t𝔼superscript𝑒𝑡superscript𝑧212𝜋superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑒𝑡superscript𝑧2superscript𝑒superscript𝑧𝐸delimited-[]𝑧22differential-d𝑧12𝜋superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑒12𝑡superscript𝑧22𝐸delimited-[]𝑧𝑧𝐸superscriptdelimited-[]𝑧22differential-d𝑧12𝜋superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑒superscript12𝑡𝑧𝐸delimited-[]𝑧2𝐸superscriptdelimited-[]𝑧212𝑡𝐸superscriptdelimited-[]𝑧22differential-d𝑧112𝑡2𝜋superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑒𝐸superscriptdelimited-[]𝑧2𝑡12𝑡superscript𝑒superscript12𝑡𝑧𝐸delimited-[]𝑧22𝑑12𝑡𝑧112𝑡superscript𝑒𝐸superscriptdelimited-[]𝑧2𝑡12𝑡\mathbb{E}e^{tz^{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{tz^{2}}e^{-\frac{\left(z-E[z]\right)^{2}}{2}}dz=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{-\frac{\left(1-2t\right)z^{2}-2E[z]z+E[z]^{2}}{2}}dz=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{-\frac{\left(\sqrt{1-2t}z-E[z]\right)^{2}-\frac{E[z]^{2}}{1-2t}+E[z]^{2}}{2}}dz=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-2t}\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{-\frac{E[z]^{2}t}{1-2t}}e^{-\frac{\left(\sqrt{1-2t}z-E[z]\right)^{2}}{2}}d\left(\sqrt{1-2t}z\right)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-2t}}e^{-\frac{E[z]^{2}t}{1-2t}}.

E​[e−t​z2]=11+2​t​e−E​[z]2​t1+2​t,t>0,formulae-sequence𝐸delimited-[]superscript𝑒𝑡superscript𝑧2112𝑡superscript𝑒𝐸superscriptdelimited-[]𝑧2𝑡12𝑡𝑡0\displaystyle E[e^{-tz^{2}}]=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+2t}}e^{\frac{-E[z]^{2}t}{1+2t}},\quad t>0, (44)

to rewrite (42) as (43). To do so, we set t=12​αx​σy2=12​σμ2​αp2αx𝑡12subscript𝛼𝑥subscriptsuperscript𝜎2𝑦12subscriptsuperscript𝜎2𝜇superscriptsubscript𝛼𝑝2subscript𝛼𝑥t=\frac{1}{2}\alpha_{x}\sigma^{2}_{y}=\frac{1}{2}\sigma^{2}_{\mu}\frac{\alpha_{p}^{2}}{\alpha_{x}}, and substitute z2=y2σy2superscript𝑧2superscript𝑦2subscriptsuperscript𝜎2𝑦z^{2}=\frac{y^{2}}{\sigma^{2}_{y}}, with y=−Φ−1​(c)​ααx+θ∗​αpαx−αpαx​μ𝑦superscriptΦ1𝑐𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥superscript𝜃subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥𝜇y=-\Phi^{-1}(c)\frac{\sqrt{\alpha}}{\alpha_{x}}+\theta^{*}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}-\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}\mu, into (42), to obtain:222222Regarding the equality in (45), we note that αpαx​12​π​∫−∞∞e−t​z2​ϕ​(μ)​𝑑μ=αpαx​12​π​∫−∞∞e−t​z2​12​π​σμ​e−(μ−E​[μ])22​σμ2​𝑑μ=αpαx​12​π​∫−∞∞e−t​z2​12​π​σμ​e−(z−E​[z])22​σμ​𝑑z=αpαx​12​π​∫−∞∞e−t​z2​12​π​e−(z−E​[z])22​𝑑zsubscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥12𝜋superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑒𝑡superscript𝑧2italic-ϕ𝜇differential-d𝜇subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥12𝜋superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑒𝑡superscript𝑧212𝜋subscript𝜎𝜇superscript𝑒superscript𝜇𝐸delimited-[]𝜇22subscriptsuperscript𝜎2𝜇differential-d𝜇subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥12𝜋superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑒𝑡superscript𝑧212𝜋subscript𝜎𝜇superscript𝑒superscript𝑧𝐸delimited-[]𝑧22subscript𝜎𝜇differential-d𝑧subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥12𝜋superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑒𝑡superscript𝑧212𝜋superscript𝑒superscript𝑧𝐸delimited-[]𝑧22differential-d𝑧\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{-tz^{2}}\phi(\mu)d\mu=\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{-tz^{2}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}\sigma_{\mu}}e^{-\frac{(\mu-E[\mu])^{2}}{2\sigma^{2}_{\mu}}}d\mu=\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{-tz^{2}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}\sigma_{\mu}}e^{-\frac{(z-E[z])^{2}}{2}}\sigma_{\mu}dz=\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{-tz^{2}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}e^{-\frac{(z-E[z])^{2}}{2}}dz. It now follows from the steps in footnote 21, equation (44) respectively, that the equality in (45) holds.

αpαx​12​π​∫−∞∞e−t​z2​ϕ​(μ)​𝑑μ=|(44)αpαx​12​π​11+αx​σy2​e−E​[y]2σy2​12​αx​σy2​11+αx​σy2\displaystyle\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}e^{-tz^{2}}\phi(\mu)d\mu=_{|(\ref{ap3})}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+\alpha_{x}\sigma_{y}^{2}}}e^{-\frac{E[y]^{2}}{\sigma^{2}_{y}}\frac{1}{2}\alpha_{x}\sigma^{2}_{y}\frac{1}{1+\alpha_{x}\sigma_{y}^{2}}} (45)
=αpαx​12​π​11+(σμ​αpαx)2​e−12​(αx​(−Φ−1​(c)​ααx+θ∗​αpαx−αpαx​E​[μ]))2​11+(σμ​αpαx)2.absentsubscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥12𝜋11superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜇subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥2superscript𝑒12superscriptsubscript𝛼𝑥superscriptΦ1𝑐𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥superscript𝜃subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥𝐸delimited-[]𝜇211superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜇subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥2\displaystyle=\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+(\sigma_{\mu}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}})^{2}}}e^{-\frac{1}{2}\Big{(}\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}\Big{(}-\Phi^{-1}(c)\frac{\sqrt{\alpha}}{\alpha_{x}}+\theta^{*}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}-\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}E[\mu]\Big{)}\Big{)}^{2}\frac{1}{1+(\sigma_{\mu}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}})^{2}}}. (46)

Where the final step from (45) to (46) involves cancelling and resubstitution of αx​σy2=(σμ​αpαx)2subscript𝛼𝑥subscriptsuperscript𝜎2𝑦superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜇subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥2\alpha_{x}\sigma^{2}_{y}=(\sigma_{\mu}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\sqrt{\alpha_{x}}})^{2} and E​[y]=−Φ−1​(c)​ααx+θ∗​αpαx−αpαx​E​[μ]𝐸delimited-[]𝑦superscriptΦ1𝑐𝛼subscript𝛼𝑥superscript𝜃subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥subscript𝛼𝑝subscript𝛼𝑥𝐸delimited-[]𝜇E[y]=-\Phi^{-1}(c)\frac{\sqrt{\alpha}}{\alpha_{x}}+\theta^{*}\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}-\frac{\alpha_{p}}{\alpha_{x}}E[\mu].

References

  • Angeletos and Werning, (2006) Angeletos, G.-M. and Werning, I. (2006). Crises and prices: Information aggregation, multiplicity, and volatility. American Economic Review, 96(5):1720–1736.
  • Atkeson, (2000) Atkeson, A. (2000). Discussion on morris and shin. In NBER Macroeconomics Annual, pages 161–170. ed. Bernanke, Ben S. and Kenneth Rogoff, Cambridge, MIT Press.
  • Carlsson and van Damme, (1993) Carlsson, H. and van Damme, E. (1993). Global games and equilibrium selection. Econometrica, 61:989–1018.
  • Dasgupta, (2007) Dasgupta, A. (2007). Coordination and delay in global games. Journal of Economic Theory, 134(1):195–225.
  • Grossman and Stiglitz, (1976) Grossman, S. and Stiglitz, J. (1976). Information and competitive price systems. American Economic Review Papers and Proceedings, 66(2):246–253.
  • Grossman and Stiglitz, (1980) Grossman, S. and Stiglitz, J. (1980). On the impossibility of informationally efficient markets. American Economic Review, 70(3):393–408.
  • Hellwig, (2002) Hellwig, C. (2002). Public information, private information, and the multiplicity of equilibria in coordination games. Journal of Economic Theory, 107:191–222.
  • Hellwig et al., (2006) Hellwig, C., Mukherji, A., and Tsyvinski, A. (2006). Self-fulfilling currency crises: The role of interest rates. American Economic Review, 95(5):1769–1787.
  • Hellwig, (1980) Hellwig, M. (1980). On the aggregation of information in competitive markets. Journal of Economic Theory, 22:477–498.
  • Izmalkov and Yildiz, (2010) Izmalkov, S. and Yildiz, M. (2010). Investor sentiments. American Economic Journal: Microeconomics, 2(1):21–38.
  • Mathevet, (2012) Mathevet, L. (2012). Beliefs and rationalizability in games with complementarities. Workingpaper, pages 1–36.
  • Metz, (2002) Metz, C. (2002). Public and private information in self-fulfilling currency crises. Journal of Economics, 76:65–85.
  • Minelli and Polemarchakis, (2003) Minelli, E. and Polemarchakis, H. (2003). Information at equilibrium. Economic Theory, 21(2-3):573–584.
  • Morris, (1995) Morris, S. (1995). The common prior assumption in economic theory. Economics and Philisophy, 11:227–253.
  • Morris and Shin, (1998) Morris, S. and Shin, H. S. (1998). Unique equilibrium in a model of self-fullfilling currency attacks. American Economic Review, 88(3):578–597.
  • Morris and Shin, (2000) Morris, S. and Shin, H. S. (2000). Rethinking multiple equilibria in macroeconomic modeling. In NBER Macroeconomics Annual, pages 139–161. ed. Bernanke, Ben S. and Kenneth Rogoff, Cambridge, MIT Press.
  • Morris and Shin, (2004) Morris, S. and Shin, H. S. (2004). Coordination risk and the price of debt. European Economic Review, 48(1):133–153.
  • Raiffa and Schlaifer, (2000) Raiffa, H. and Schlaifer, R. (2000). Applied Statistical Decision Theory. Wiley Classics Library.
  • Rao, (1965) Rao, C. R. (1965). Linear Statistical Inference and its Applications. Wiley, New York.
  • Rubinstein, (1989) Rubinstein, A. (1989). The electronic mail game: Strategic behaviour under ’almost common knowledge’. American Economic Review, 79(3):385–391.
  • Sethi and Yildiz, (2012) Sethi, R. and Yildiz, M. (2012). Public disagreement. American Economic Journal: Microeconomics, 4(3):57–95.
  • Steiner and Stewart, (2008) Steiner, J. and Stewart, C. (2008). Contagion through learning. Theoretical Economics, 3:431–458.
